---
title: "Collections"
subtitle: "Ideas gathered by mathematical landscape rather than by format."
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

::: {.content-block .mn-page-lead}
A collection may contain an interactive, an essay, a problem sequence and classroom notes. This makes it possible to revisit one idea at different levels of formality and from different points of view.
:::

::: {.content-block .mn-collection-grid}
::: {.mn-collection-card .mn-collection-live}
<p class="mn-kicker">ACTIVE COLLECTION</p>

## Kolams, tiles and sliding orbits

Binary encodings, edge constraints, graph connectivity, enumeration and the parity structure of the 15-puzzle.

[Explore the collection →](../explore/index.qmd)
:::

::: {.mn-collection-card}
<p class="mn-kicker">PLANNED</p>

## Geometry in motion

Configurations that reveal their structure only when a point, line or circle is allowed to move.
:::

::: {.mn-collection-card}
<p class="mn-kicker">PLANNED</p>

## Algebra made visible

Roots of unity, symmetry and transformations viewed through diagrams and computation.
:::

::: {.mn-collection-card}
<p class="mn-kicker">PLANNED</p>

## Problems that grow

Olympiad-style questions extended into families, variants and open-ended investigations.
:::
:::
